name: Aurelius
description: The Imperial Capital. Grand colonnades and marble halls still impress, but cracks run through the foundations — both literal and figurative.
rumors: The Emperor hasn't been seen in public for weeks
Tax collectors are demanding double this season

name: Crosswall
description: The western frontier fortress. Its walls have held for centuries, but the garrison grows thinner each year as soldiers go unpaid.
rumors: Half the garrison deserted last month
Something was spotted moving in the western hills

name: Kingsbridge
description: A vital trade town at the great river crossing. Merchants, travelers, and opportunists all pass through here.
rumors: Bandits were spotted on the south road
A merchant claims to have maps to a lost vault

name: Valdris Hold
description: The seat of House Valdris. Well-fortified and well-supplied, it grows stronger while the empire weakens.
rumors: Lord Valdris has been hiring sellswords from across the sea
New forges burn day and night

name: Millhaven
description: A prosperous farming town under Valdris protection. The mills turn constantly, and the granaries are always full.
rumors: Valdris soldiers drill in the fields every morning
A traveling priest warned of dark omens

name: Thornefield
description: The modest seat of House Thorne. Not grand, but lovingly maintained. The people here feel safe — for now.
rumors: Lady Thorne trains with her soldiers every dawn
Goblin tracks were found near the southern woods

name: Shepherd's Rest
description: A quiet village of sheep farmers and honest folk. They sleep well knowing House Thorne watches over them — but Thornefield is a long ride away.
rumors: Wolves have been acting strangely in the hills
Old Tom swears he saw goblin scouts at dusk

name: Blackhollow
description: A ruined fort claimed by the Iron Fang. Scar has made it functional — barely. But it's defensible, and that's what matters.
rumors: New faces arrive at Blackhollow every week
Scar was seen talking to a hooded stranger

name: Greentusk Caves
description: A sprawling cavern network that serves as the Greentusk Horde's stronghold. The tunnels go deeper than anyone has mapped.
rumors: Grak has been forging alliances with other goblin tribes
Strange drums echo from the deep caves at night

name: Ashwood
description: Once a thriving village, now charred timbers and ash. The goblins burned it last spring. A warning to all.

name: Marketcross
description: The busiest trading hub in the region. Coin flows freely and so does information. The Silver Road Guild calls it home.
rumors: A foreign merchant is buying up all the iron
The Guild has hired a new company of guards

name: Oldford
description: A farming village at a shallow ford. Close enough to Blackhollow to worry, far enough from help to feel alone.
rumors: Iron Fang riders have been spotted on the far bank
The ford runs red after heavy rains — probably just clay

name: Haven Chapel
description: A monastery and place of healing. Respected by all factions as neutral ground. The monks tend to anyone who comes in peace.
rumors: A wounded knight arrived last week and hasn't spoken since
The abbot has been having troubling visions

name: Irondeep Mine
description: A rich iron mine that has supplied the empire for generations. Increasingly dangerous as the tunnels go deeper.
rumors: Miners broke into an old chamber last week — something was already inside
Production is down and the foreman won't say why

name: Dusthaven
description: A remote village far from any protection. The people here are self-reliant by necessity. If trouble comes, they're on their own.
rumors: Strange lights were seen on the moor at midnight
A peddler hasn't returned from the east road

name: Raven's Watch
description: A signal tower on the northern ridge. Once part of a chain of beacons, now mostly abandoned. One old keeper tends the flame.
rumors: The keeper claims he saw an army on the northern horizon
Ravens gather here in unnatural numbers

name: Willowmere
description: A lakeside village known for fishing and folk tales. Peaceful on the surface, but the lake holds old secrets.
rumors: Fish have been dying in the northern shallows
An old woman claims the lake spirit is restless

name: Gallows Hill
description: An abandoned execution ground from a harsher era. Nobody goes here willingly. Even bandits avoid it after dark.

name: Brightwater
description: A coastal town thriving on fishing and sea trade. Far enough from the frontier to feel safe — for now.
rumors: Strange ships have been seen offshore
The harbormaster is taking bribes to look the other way

name: The Maw
description: A deep chasm that splits the earth. Ancient stairs descend into darkness. Something stirs in the depths — old, patient, and growing stronger.
rumors: The ground trembles near The Maw
A scholar went in three months ago. His torch was found at the entrance, still burning

name: Imperial Catacombs
description: Beneath the capital lies a vast network of burial chambers, dating back to the founding dynasty. The dead outnumber the living here a thousand to one.
rumors: Grave robbers have been disappearing in the lower levels
The imperial seals on the deepest vaults have been broken

name: Dragonspine Ruins
description: The shattered remains of an ancient fortress built along a rocky ridge. Legend says a dragon was slain here in the Age of Heroes. The bones in the walls might not all be stone.
rumors: Treasure hunters occasionally emerge with ancient coins
At night, the wind through the ruins sounds like breathing

name: The Bonepile
description: An ossuary beneath Gallows Hill where centuries of executed criminals were dumped. Iron Fang scouts use the tunnels as escape routes. Something else uses them too.
rumors: Scar forbids his people from going deeper than the third level
The bones are rearranging themselves

name: The Fungal Depths
description: Below the Greentusk Caves, where even goblins fear to tread. Bioluminescent fungi cast an eerie glow over vast caverns. The air itself is toxic to the unprepared.
rumors: Goblin shamans harvest rare ingredients from the upper reaches
Something immense moves in the deepest chambers

name: The Sunken Shrine
description: A half-submerged temple at the river delta, sacred to a forgotten god. Pilgrims still come, drawn by dreams they cannot explain.
rumors: The water level in the shrine rises and falls with no relation to the river
A hermit priest claims the god is waking

name: The Whispering Barrows
description: Ancient burial mounds from a civilization that predates the empire. On still nights, voices can be heard from within — speaking in a language no scholar recognizes.
rumors: A Valdris archaeologist was found wandering nearby, unable to remember her own name
The grass on the barrows never grows higher than an inch

name: Stormhaven
description: A sheltered harbor on the eastern coast. Valdris ships dock here, and foreign traders bring exotic goods. The lighthouse keeper swears the storms are getting worse.
rumors: A foreign fleet was spotted on the horizon last week
Smugglers operate openly after dark

name: Western Outpost
description: The empire's most remote garrison, clinging to a mountain pass. The soldiers here haven't been paid in three months. Desertion is a matter of when, not if.
rumors: The commander has been sending increasingly desperate messages to Crosswall
Something large has been testing the palisade at night

name: Northwall
description: A crumbling watchtower on the empire's northern frontier. The soldiers scan an empty horizon and wonder if they've been forgotten.
rumors: Tracks of an unknown creature were found in the snow
The night watch reported seeing fires in the northern wastes
